title: podman image trust overhaul, incl. sigstore
body:
See release note.

<s>WIP because all of this would benefit from unit test coverage.</s>

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
- BREAKING CHANGE: `podman image trust show` may now show multiple entries for the same scope, to better represent separate requirements. GPG IDs on a single row now always represent alternative keys, only one of which is required; if multiple sets of keys are required, each is represented by a single line.
- Fixed `podman image trust set` not to silently discard unknown fields.
- Added support for `sigstoreSigned` to `podman image trust set`
- Added support for `sigstoreSigned` to `podman image trust show`
- Updated `podman image trust show` to recognize new `lookaside` field names
- Updated `podman image trust show` to recognize `keyPaths` in `signedBy` entries
- Fixed `podman image trust show` to potentially show signature enforcement configuration for the default scope
- Fixed `podman image trust show` to not silently ignore multiple kinds of requirements in a single scope
```

comments.body:
This is now ready for review. Note the BREAKING CHANGE release note. I think that’s inevitable so that we can reasonably represent
- (key1 OR key2) AND key3, which is now much more likely to in default RHEL configurations with `keyPaths` accepting both a production and beta key for the RH registries.
- that a repo must be signed _both_ using simple signing and sigstore.

Until now, `podman image trust` was silently discarding unknown fields, which is rather bad. E.g. Podman 4.2’s `podman image trust` would remove the `keyPaths` field used in the default configuration.

---

See individual commits for details. 

Most of the logic was moved from `pkg/domain/infra/abi/trust.go` to `pkg/trust`, where it can be cheaply unit-tested for quick iteration.

The unit tests now also test interoperability with the c/image/v5/signature parser and formatter.

---

This is admittedly way too much for a single PR — I wanted to have good unit test coverage (so that there is any confidence in the code), and that was easier to do all in a single batch.

I’d be happy to split this into smaller parts, and especially to discuss which parts of this we _need_ for 4.2 backports. I’d recommend at least
- “podman image trust set` does not discard unknown data” (basic hygiene; minimum alternative is to add the `keyPaths` field)
- “support sigstoreSigned in `podman image trust show`” (to avoid ugly warnings, but otherwise not critical)
- “support `lookaside` in `podman image trust show`” (we are now shipping it in default configuration)
- “support `keyPaths` in `podman image trust show`” (we are now shipping it in default configuration, but it brings along the question of the breaking change multi-line output)

but of course it would be easier for me to add the whole bunch as is.

---

Cc: @TomSweeneyRedHat for a discussion of timing and how much of this we want to backport to 4.2 — note the list above (and the release note at the top), we are changing the default configuration in a way that is not currently supposed by `podman image trust`, either showing or even writing the wrong thing.
